title: fagraea curtisii var. calcarea
intro: Fagraea curtisii var. calcarea, also known as the calcareous Fagraea, is a plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae, which is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Fagraea curtisii var. calcarea are attributed to its alkaloid compounds, including fagraein and fagron, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a traditional remedy for wound healing and skin conditions. Fagraea curtisii var. calcarea can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
